requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate catecholamine-related tachyarrhythmia or severe hypertension complication. Arrhythmia changes monitoring urgency and perioperative safety planning.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets catecholamine-related tachyarrhythmia or severe hypertension complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Pheochromocytoma.
requiredExam.technique: Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When palpitations or catecholamine excess are possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: catecholamine-related tachyarrhythmia or severe hypertension complication
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Arrhythmia changes monitoring urgency and perioperative safety planning.
requiredExam.limitations: Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Pheochromocytoma, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.action: Add when vomiting, dehydration, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia is present; this evaluates volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Pheochromocytoma.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When vomiting, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia, hypercalcemia, or hypernatremia is present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Pheochromocytoma, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Test with plasma free metanephrines or urinary fractionated metanephrines using proper sampling conditions; repeat borderline results with optimized preparation.
Marked metanephrine elevation, especially >3 times upper limit, strongly supports PPGL and should route to localization imaging.
Do not proceed to biopsy or surgery for suspected PPGL without biochemical framing and preoperative blockade unless emergent specialist-directed care is required.
Assess hereditary risk, multifocal disease, metastatic disease, and medication/stress confounders.
Confirm that Pheochromocytoma is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.

Order the first-line tests in sequence: Plasma free metanephrines or urinary fractionated metanephrines; Use proper sampling conditions; supine rest for plasma when possible; CT/MRI only after biochemical evidence.
Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Begin alpha-adrenergic blockade and volume/salt preparation before surgery once diagnosis is established; add beta-blocker only after adequate alpha blockade if tachyarrhythmia requires it.
Refer for adrenalectomy or tumor resection with endocrine, anesthesia, and surgical planning.
Use genetic counseling/testing when PPGL is confirmed or hereditary features are present.
Manage hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, ACS/stroke symptoms, or planned urgent procedure with specialist/ICU-level support.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Pheochromocytoma; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Pheochromocytoma, choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.

clinical_cutoff_review.review_note: Cutoff rows were added because the prior module evidence did not expose enough explicit guideline thresholds for pathway traversal.
